name: Simple Progress Bar

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Simple Progress Bar 0.2.0

This release prepares the plugin for public Obsidian distribution and adds the current polished progress-bar experience.

### Highlights
- Adds note-wide checkbox progress in the note header, updated as tasks are checked or unchecked.
- Adds section-specific progress bars with `sp-bar` code blocks.
- Adds command palette actions to insert an inline progress bar and show, hide, or toggle the note progress bar.
- Supports selected text as the label when inserting an embedded progress bar.
- Adds a 100% completion animation and cleaner theme-adaptive styling.

### Release-readiness fixes
- Updates release metadata to `0.2.0` with `minAppVersion` `0.15.0`.
- Keeps the plugin mobile-compatible (`isDesktopOnly: false`) and avoids Node/Electron-only runtime APIs.
- Removes system clipboard usage from the settings UI, eliminating the clipboard-access review warning.
- Adds release/mobile validation scripts and a GitHub release workflow that builds, uploads, and attests `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`.
- Addresses Obsidian review warnings around unsafe DOM patterns and module usage.
- Improves README usage, command, troubleshooting, and manual-installation docs.

### Known issue
- Obsidian may delay re-rendering a freshly edited `sp-bar` code block until the note view refreshes. If an embedded bar does not appear immediately after editing the code block itself, switch notes or toggle reading/source mode once.
